title: App Shell — Navigation, Dark Theme, Onboarding, Empty States
problem: The app needs a solid foundation: a responsive web app that works on any device, navigates between recording/editing/collaboration screens fluidly, enforces auth correctly, handles first-time setup (create a band, link a Drive folder) with minimal friction, and never shows a blank screen.
analysis: Next.js App Router. No PWA manifest in V1 — standard responsive web app only. Dark theme only — no light mode in V1 (background #0a0a0a, accent amber #f59e0b). System fonts only (-apple-system/BlinkMacSystemFont/Segoe UI — no custom font loading). Bottom tab bar (4 tabs: Sessions, Record, Clips, Band) with the following route hierarchy: / (landing) → /login → /bands (home) → /bands/[bandId] (session list) → /bands/[bandId]/sessions/[sessionId] (clip list) → /bands/[bandId]/sessions/[sessionId]/clips/[clipId] (clip detail: version list + activity) → /bands/[bandId]/sessions/[sessionId]/clips/[clipId]/edit (timeline editor, full-screen, tab bar hidden) → /record (recording screen, full-screen takeover, tab bar hidden) → /share/[token] (public frozen clip, no auth, no tabs). Tab bar is hidden during active recording and in the timeline editor. First-run flow: sign in → if no bands, show 'Create your band' wizard (name → creates Drive folder → creates Postgres band record → invite QR shown). Empty states for all zero-content views — no blank screens.
successCriteria:
- Dark theme renders correctly everywhere; no light mode; high contrast readable in dim rehearsal room or car
- System fonts only — no web font loading
- Bottom tab bar: Sessions / Record / Clips / Band — always visible except recording screen and timeline editor
- All interactive elements have touch targets >= 44px
- Unauthenticated users land on / and see sign-in only
- First-run: new user with no bands sees 'Create band' wizard immediately after sign-in
- /share/[token] is public — no auth required, no private data exposed
- Back navigation works correctly on mobile (browser back button)
- Recording screen accessible from tab bar in 1 tap from any screen
- Empty state — no sessions in band: 'No sessions yet. Hit Record to capture your first jam.'
- Empty state — no clips in session: 'Nothing recorded yet for this session.'
- Empty state — no versions on clip (just v1): 'v1 is the original. Open the editor to make your first cut.'
- Empty state — no bands: triggers first-run wizard (never a dead end)
- Loading skeletons shown while data is fetching (session list, clip list, version list, waveform)
implementation:
1. app/layout.tsx: root layout with dark theme CSS variables, system font stack, <html className='dark'>.
2. components/layout/BottomTabBar.tsx: client component — 4 tabs (Sessions, Record, Clips, Band). Hidden on /record and /edit routes via usePathname(). Record tab shows pulsing ring when recording in progress.
3. app/(auth)/layout.tsx: authenticated route group layout wrapping all protected pages.
4. app/(auth)/bands/page.tsx: home screen — lists user's bands. Redirects to /setup if no bands found.
5. app/setup/page.tsx: first-run wizard — create band name, link Drive folder, show invite QR.
6. components/ui/EmptyState.tsx: reusable empty state card with icon, title, description, optional action button.
7. components/ui/Button.tsx, ButtonLink.tsx: shared button components.
8. components/ui/BottomSheet.tsx: reusable bottom drawer for sheets.
9. components/ui/InlineEdit.tsx: in-place editable text field.
10. tailwind.config.ts: dark theme color tokens (base, surface, elevated, accent, danger, primary, secondary, muted, border).
11. app/globals.css: CSS custom properties for all color tokens.

decisions.decision: No PWA in V1 — standard responsive web app only
decisions.rationale: PWA manifest + service worker caching adds complexity (update flow, cache invalidation, iOS limitations) without a compelling V1 benefit. The app is used on a browser in the car or at home — bookmarking is sufficient. PWA can be added later if add-to-home-screen demand is validated.
decisions.recommendation: No next-pwa, no manifest.json, no service worker registration in V1. Standard Next.js build.

decisions.rationale: Primary use context is dim rehearsal rooms and cars at night. A light theme would be jarring. No user-configurable theme in V1 — dark is the only mode. Background #0a0a0a, accent amber #f59e0b for recording/active states.
decisions.recommendation: Tailwind dark mode via class strategy on <html>. CSS custom properties for all color tokens. No toggle control.

decisions.rationale: Users need to jump to Record from anywhere in one tap — that's the most critical action in a live jam context. Bottom tabs keep Record always reachable. The tab bar is hidden during full-screen experiences (recording, timeline editor) where it would clutter the primary task.
decisions.recommendation: Four tabs: Sessions, Record, Clips, Band. Tab bar visible on all standard screens, hidden on /record and /edit routes. Record tab shows pulsing amber ring when recording in progress.

decisions.decision: System fonts only — no custom font loading
decisions.rationale: App is used in dark, low-bandwidth settings (post-jam, car). Custom fonts add bundle size, FOUT risk, and load time for zero perceptible benefit. System fonts are instantly available, legible, and familiar.
decisions.recommendation: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif. Monospace: ui-monospace for timestamps and durations only.

decisions.decision: First-run band wizard on initial sign-in
decisions.rationale: A new user with no bands cannot do anything useful. Rather than leaving them on an empty home screen, detect the zero-bands state and immediately show 'Create your first band' with Drive folder setup inline. Reduces time-to-first-recording.
decisions.recommendation: After sign-in, check Postgres for user's band memberships. If zero: redirect to /setup instead of /bands.

knownLimitations: No add-to-home-screen in V1 — users must bookmark the app in their browser
userGuide: After signing in, users land on their bands list. The bottom tab bar (Sessions / Record / Clips / Band) is always visible. Tap Record to go directly to the recording screen from anywhere. On first sign-in with no bands, the app redirects to a setup wizard to create the first band. Empty states guide users to the next action at every dead end — no blank screens.
